channel.title: OOIR Trends: Clinical Medicine (Integrative & Complementary Medicine)
channel.description: Aktuelle Paper-Trends im Bereich Clinical Medicine (Integrative & Complementary Medicine) von OOIR (mit Titel und Metadaten von Crossref)

item.title: Role of an Ayurvedic formulation ‘Kalyanaka Ghrita’ in mild cognitive impairment: A single-patient, quasi-randomized, controlled, open-label N-of-1 trial
item.description: Feld: Clinical Medicine Kategorie: Integrative & Complementary Medicine Score: 13 Autoren: Swati Sharma, Arun Kaushik, Sankha Shubhra Chakrabarti, Kishor Patwardhan Journal: Journal of Ethnopharmacology Veröffentlicht: 2026-12-01 DOI: 10.1016/j.jep.2026.122089 ISSN: 0378-8741 Tag der Erhebung (OOIR): 2026-07-09

item.title: Ditan Decoction alleviates glutamate excitotoxicity in an Aβ-induced Alzheimer's disease-like model through the regulation of ERBB2/PI3K/AKT signaling pathway
item.description: Feld: Clinical Medicine Kategorie: Integrative & Complementary Medicine Score: 7 Autoren: Lisong Ren, Shangzhi Liu, Xingyang Sun, Yini Zhang, Ping Wang, Ling Liu, Lin-Lin Chen Journal: Journal of Ethnopharmacology Veröffentlicht: 2026-12-01 DOI: 10.1016/j.jep.2026.122084 ISSN: 0378-8741 Tag der Erhebung (OOIR): 2026-07-09

item.title: Targeting the Nrf2 Signaling Pathway: A Review of Traditional Chinese Medicine for Alzheimer’s Disease
item.description: Feld: Clinical Medicine Kategorie: Integrative & Complementary Medicine Score: 7 Autoren: Ming Liu, Hang Hong, Xingxing Zhang, Jiayu Ren, Chunlan Tang Journal: The American Journal of Chinese Medicine Veröffentlicht: 2026-01-01 Abstract: Alzheimer’s disease (AD) is a progressive neurodegenerative disorder primarily characterized by β-amyloid (Aβ) deposition, Tau protein hyperphosphorylation, and chronic neuroinflammation. Current pharmacological interventions demonstrate limited therapeutic efficacy. The nuclear factor erythroid 2-related factor 2 (Nrf2) signaling pathway, a pivotal regulator of oxidative stress and neuroinflammation, plays a critical role in AD pathogenesis. Recent studies have revealed that traditional Chinese medicines (TCMs) and their bioactive constituents can modulate the Nrf2 signaling pathway to mitigate oxidative stress, suppress neuroinflammation, enhance Aβ clearance, and reduce Tau protein phosphorylation. By doing so, TCMs exert multi-targeted anti-AD effects. This review systematically summarizes the mechanisms and recent advances concerning the active ingredients of Nrf2 pathway-modulating TCMs, herbal medicines, and TCM formulations for the prevention and treatment of AD. Furthermore, it critically evaluates current research limitations and prospects for future research directions to provide a theoretical foundation for the development of novel anti-AD therapeutics derived from TCMs. DOI: 10.1142/s0192415x26500400 ISSN: 0192-415X Tag der Erhebung (OOIR): 2026-07-09

item.title: Huang-Lian-Jie-Du Decoction alleviates cognitive deficits in Alzheimer’s disease via aromatase-mediated regulation of Aβ metabolism
item.description: Feld: Clinical Medicine Kategorie: Integrative & Complementary Medicine Score: 7 Autoren: Mingguang Sun, Dongsheng Ye, Puxuan Min, Mengxiong Guo, Xiao Zhao, Yue Zhou, Lingxu Xu, Yutong Wu, Zhaoyou Meng, Gesheng Wang Journal: Phytomedicine Veröffentlicht: 2026-09-01 DOI: 10.1016/j.phymed.2026.158467 ISSN: 0944-7113 Tag der Erhebung (OOIR): 2026-07-09

item.title: Manual therapy treatment mechanisms are complex: Challenges and a call to action
item.description: Feld: Clinical Medicine Kategorie: Integrative & Complementary Medicine Score: 7 Autoren: Chad E. Cook, Damian Keter, Vitaly Napadow, Mary F. Barbe, Stephen M. Perle, Jennifer Bent, Kenneth Learman, William R. Reed Journal: Complementary Therapies in Medicine Veröffentlicht: 2026-09-01 DOI: 10.1016/j.ctim.2026.103393 ISSN: 0965-2299 Tag der Erhebung (OOIR): 2026-07-09
